ACURA «HONDA - Transponder Information ;
| ILCO / JET / STRATTEC CLONING KEYS
There can be a lot of confusion concerning which is the right key to use and what is the purpose of all the different after-mar-
ket Acura/Honda & GM transponder keys. Let me explain what it all means, 7
It's all in the part number and type of transponder chip. Cloning transponder keys have a chip in them that are made to be have _
the transponder ID read and also written too. Cloning transponder keys require a machine like the JET "EDT-1" or the SILCA "AW2" to read
the chip value, which is usually 32 digits long. The cloning machines read the value of the original chip key and than can write that value
onto a after-market cloning chip key, :
Here's one of the problems with cloning transponder keys. They all come with a "Zero" value, Meaning the 32-digit value is all
Zero's. During programming of a new key into the car's memory the car's immobilizer computer is looking for a unique value to the chip _
and will not accept-a value of all zeros, The car's immobilizer computer see's this as an error and in turn causes a fault to be registered in
the PCM (Powertrain Control Module) computer. To avoid doing this and creating more problems pay close attention to the part numbers :
that ILCO and JET use, Strattec "RW" keys are cloning keys.
 - | The "PHT" means :
| The "N" means neutral value, which
| is the same as Zero value, z
- : : ie The "PT" means
ILCO Key Example: Clone Type- HD106-PT5 | Plastic (Bow) Transponder |
: =. |
| The "5" means this is a Zero value. |
STRATTEC key Example: Clone Type - "RW" means Read Write

On-Board Remote Programming
 © Note! You will need to have any other Remotes / Remote Head Keys with you otherwise they
_ will get erased Once you start the procedure: = _ a ;
; 1) __ Sit inside the vehicle with all doors closed.
2) Insert the-key and turn it to the ON position:. Press the Lock button ona unpro-
3 grammed Remote Head key.
3) Turn the. key to the OFF position. Turn back ON the key a 2nd time'and press the Lock
 button again.
4) Turn the key to the OFF position. Turn back ON the key a 3rd time and press the Lock
button again.
"5) Turn the key to the OFF position, Turn back ON the key a 4th time and press the Lock
: butten-again. :
You stould now hear and see the door locks cycle once, Keep the key turned ON.
6) Press the Lock button on each Remote Head you want to program into the vehicle,
You should now hear and see the door locks cycle once.
Important Notes: - ; %
= 1} You must Gomplete each step.within 5 seconds, :
=> 2) To start out the procedure to get into programming mode you must use the same
Remote each of the 4 times.

 EMERGENCY ENGINE START PROCEDURE:
Models: CL, TL & INTEGRA & MDX- Use the Brake Pedal - -
Models: 3.5RL; NSX,.ASX & TSX = Usethe Hand Brake
1. Make a mechanical key that will work the ignition lock cylinder,
4 z. Have the customer get the "Immobilizer Brake Code" from the dealer or Honda Customer Relations, you
will need the Vehicle Identification Number. This is a five digit number code. Each car has a different
"immobilizer Brake Code" since they are VIN specific,
3, Tum the ignition switch to the ON position (II), ;

4, Have the BRAKE CODE in front of you. To enter the first number of the BRAKE CODE, press and release
the brake the'same number of times as the first number in the BRAKE CODE. For.example if the first
number in the BRAKE CODE is 2, press and release the brake 2 times. :

5, Jurn:the ignition switch te the LOCK position.

6. Within 30 seconds, turn the ignition switch to the ON position (Il). For example if the 2nd number in the

ae . BRAKE CODE is a3, press and release the brake 3 times. -
_ 7 Continué entering the BRAKE CODE as described above for the rest 3rd; 4th & Sth digits.
After entering the 5th number, the Immobilizer Indicator light on the dash:should flash 5 times and then
= = -oghut off, The car should now start. This procedure must be repeated each time.you turn off the car for more
4 than 10 minutes. The Immobilizer System indicator will remain on while the engine is 'running, :

IMPORTANT NOTE: If the customer finds his/her keys or-drives home to get them, they cannot.use
them-until the vehicle has been turned off for more than 10 minutes.' .

ba Facts to remember: ;
bss 1. All tumblers:are numbered, but are reverse of normal depths, a number 5 is a shallow cutand a number 1 depth is the
: Er deepest cut. :
Be 2, Tumbler position 8R is always a number 4 depth for the master key, and a number 2 depth for a valet key. This is the only
_ ee. : different between a master and a valet key.
fe 3. Tumblers 4L, 3L, 21, 1L,and 6L are not used in-any locks, but must follaw MACS. (So the key works smoothly}
Kr 4, Because of the tolerances of the center groove you can not havea number 5 depth on the left track opposite a number
eae 4 or 5 depth, There wouldn't be enough room for the key to pass down in the keyway of the lock (and vice-versa).
| _|__ 5. Thereis never a number 1depth in the Sth, 6th, 7th, or Bth position from the bow, if there is one at all. Either Left or Right.
Ss Method #1 Get key code from-dealer and make key by code. The dealer can retrieve the key code for the Millenia hy
> entering the VIN into their national:computer hook-up withthe factory. -
Ss Method #2 The Millenia has an electric trunk release on the driver's. door, There is an on/off switch for the electric trunk
= release in the top section of the glove box. Open the trunk and remove the cylinder. Disassemble and
_ Q decode, to make master key, since all the tumblers needed are located in the trunk lock.

: Transponder Information - MAZDA
MAZDA CAN TYPE SYSTEMS: MAZDA 3, MAZDA 6 & RX-8:
> These cars.use the C.A.N. system that requies an OUTCODE that you need to get converted from the dealer into
anINCODE, The OUTCODE is always:6 digits and the INCODE is always 4 digits.

FORD TYPE SYSTEMS: PICK UPS & TRIBUTE

> The B-Series Pick Ups and the Tribute models 'are actually Ford style transponder systerns, The B-Series Pick Up
is the brother to the Ford Ranger. The Tribute is the brother to the Ford Escape. A T-Code or NGS device is needed
for programming. No PIN number is needed.

MAZDA 626:- MIATA - MPV: ~~

mi > The "immobilizer Control Unit" can hold up to 8 registered (recognized) keys. Ifa new key has to be programmed
in tothe car's Immobilizer system, all other keys will be erased. Therefor, all available keys will need to be present for
programming. The Immebilizer Unit and PCM (Power Control Module) cannot be taken off and Used on other cars
and visa-versa. |f there are less than 2 pre-programmed transponder keys, a password will be needed to open the

= Immobilizer Unit's programming mode.
> For ears without the Immobilizer system, the ignition face will have the large words "LOCK, "ACC, "ON! "START"
stamped (molded) into the metal gray face, as with other Mazda products;
> For cars with the Immobilizer system, the ignition face will look much smaller and the words "LOCK", "ACC, "ON"
"START" will be stamped (molded) into the black induction coil itself. z
>To program in keys you will need the serial # off the transponder box mounted near the right side of the steering
column in. most cases. The dealercan provide the "CodeWord" which is needed to:program a new transponder
_key. You can use factory keys for programming, or "8C" keys that 'are pre-programmed to work on these vehicles.
No special equipment needed. Follow the instructions on entering the CodeWord into the cars computer, in the
following pages, ?

USE THIS PROCEDURE FOR WHEN THERE ARE NO KEYS OR ONLY 1
PRE-PROGRAMMED KEY AND YOU NEED TO PROGRAM MORE KEYS: :
| IMPORTANT: YOU CAN USE FACTORY KEYS or "8C" KEYS THAT ARE 7
PRE-PROGRAMMED TO WORK WITH THIS SYSTEM. |
1 Cut the mechanical cuts on each new transponder key.
aig : 2 : el
5 Insert the first key and turn the ignition to the ON position and then back to the
2 LOCK position, five times. You cannot leave the key in the ON or LOCK position
 for more than one second. Do not turn the key too fast or it will not register.
Lee fe : = = abel. _
Turn the key back to the ON position and watch the flashing security light onthe |
| | dash. The light should flash rapidly, less than 1 second on and off.
|
= ! = = = C 5 ee
 | Within 20 seconds, turn the key to the LOCK position for 5 minutes, AfterS
4 | minutes the security light should decrease in flashing to 1.2 seconds between
| flashes.
5 Within 20 seconds, start to. enter the PINCODE (password), (See NEXT PAGE.)
|
38 - MAZDA @1994, 2021 AutoSmart by Michael Hyde
Transponder Information - MAZDA -

'98-'02 626, '00-'05 MIATA & MPV Transponder
How to enter "PINCODE' Procedure

The PINCODE can be obtained by retrieving the serial number off the >)

immobilizer Unit'and then converting it with the MVP or TCODE or getting it converted |
from the Dealer, The PINCODE has eight numbers. ay

-_-_--
|
|

A Before you start, the security lig ht on the dash should be flashing on and off for 1.2
seconds each, !

IB | For this purpose, let's say the PINCODE is "28681457! Below is an example.

| The first digit in the PINCODE is a'2:
Cc | Turn the ignition key to the ON position while the security light is not illuminated.
~ Count the illumination flashes TWO times and while the light is not illuminated
turn the key to the LOCK position. (This is an example.)

The second digit in the PINCODE is a '3. > ee
7 Within 30 seconds of turning the key to the LOCK position, turn the ignition key to
D the ON position while the security light is not illuminated. Count the illumination
flashes THREE times and while the light is not illuminated turn the key to the
LOCK position.

E | Repeat the above step to enter the rest of the PINCODE,

|. After you have entered the last number, turn the ignition to LOCK and then back
 = ON. Once you have entered the PINCODE correctly, the security light will stop

flashing and stay illuminated. If you make a mistake, just wait 45.seconds arid start
over, Do notremove the key.

©1994, 2027 AutoSmart by Michael Hyde MAZDA - 39
| MAZDA - Transponder Information
'98-'02 626, '00-'05 MIATA & MPV Transponder
Follow the next steps after you have entered the PINCODE
When you have entered the PINCODE correctly, the security light should stay on -_|
and not flash.
 The car should now start. Start the car and the security light should stay lit for |
between 1 to 2 seconds,
| -s = - - 7]
| If you are going to add a second key for programming, within 20 seconds, remove
 the first key and insert the second key and start the car. The security light should
 stay lit for between 1 to 2 seconds. |
4 Stop if you have no more keys to program.
5 You must wait 30 seconds to.end the program mode. |
| IMPORTANT INFO:
| > The ignition key shou id not be turned ON twice within one non-illumination cycle.
| > The ignition key should not be turned ON during an illumination cycle.
 > The ignition key should not be turned to LOCK twice within one non-illumination cycle. |

a deepest cut.

bas. orS depth. There wouldn't-be enough room forthe key te pass down inthe keyway of the lock (and visa-versa)
»= | _ § There is never a number 1 depth in any position but the 4th position from the bow, if there is one at all. Either 4L or 4R.

On-Board Programming with Existing Keys

A Chrysler transponder key once programmed into one car can not be programmed to work any
other car, but the it can re-programmed back into the same vehicle if it got erased,

The transponder code identification is learned by the Sentry Key Immobilizer Module during
programming. The Sentry Key Immobilizer Module sends the "Secret Code" to the transponder
key and is retained by the transponder key, during programming. After programming of the
transponder key identification to the Sentry Key Immobilizer Module and the Secret Code is
exchanged, the Sentry Key Immobilizer Module will ask for the transponder identification and the
Secret Code every time the key is turned to ON or START.

No Emergency "Start" procedures for Chrysler vehicles.

ADD A KEY WHEN YOU ALREADY HAVE TWO:
(Two existing pre-programmed keys must be present for this next method.)

1, Insert one of the pre-programmed transponder keys into the ignition lock cylinder and turn the
switch to the ON position.

2. The key should remain in the ON position for 5 seconds but not more than 15 seconds before
you turn it back to the OFF position.

3. Within 10 seconds, insert the second pre-programmed transponder key into the ignition lock
cylinder and turn the switch to the .ON position. The VTSS (Vehicle Theft Security System) or
Sentry Key Immobilizer System lamp (depending on model) should start to flash, This along
with a audible tone will indicate to you that the system has entered the "Customer Learn"
programming mode.

4, Within 45 seconds, turn the switch to the OFF position. Insert the new transponder key and
turn the switch to the ON position. After approximately 10 seconds an audible tone will sound
and the Indicator lamp will stay illuminated for 3 seconds. This will be your acknowledgement
that you have successfully programmed in the new transponder key.

5. To add additional keys, repeat steps 1 through 4.

| 4/1). Putthe newly cut transponder key into the keyway and turn it tothe ON (RUN) position. A theft indicator light on the

 | 4). When the light.stops flashing for the third time, the new key is programmed into. the computer and will start the car:

| Service Note:
| | | Some 2000-03 Focus style ignitions have a tendency to fail. Ignition lock cylinder removal with a lock
_ | | that was working one moment and stops the next is common,
_| | The method | use to remove the ignition is to first cut a mechanical key'and than cut the bow off the key |
| | tight before the area where the first cut would be. Insert that cut blade all the way into the lock cylinder
jae until it-bottoms out, | then take a very large Craftsmen screwdriver and insert it into the keyway and 7
reck it back and forth to get the lock to turn on,
 Once the lock is turned to the ON position you can depress the active retainer and remove the cylinder "7
to replace this inexpensive part. if the cylinder does not turn ON you.can snap the face of the cylinder
 off using the large Craftsmen screwdriver. Once the face of the ignition cylinder is removed you will _~
'| | have the side bar and tumblers exposed,
"| | Pull out the sidebar and turn the keyway to the ON position, to remove the cylinder,
|| Use ASP # C-42-188 as a replacement ignition service pack, ASP keying kit # A-42-111 will be needed, |

es | V.AT.S. (Vehicle Anti-Theft System) |
Fae Introduced first on the 1886 Chevy Corvette and then later on mare models as an anti-theft system for the ignition
 >= starting system. A pellet is embedded in the ignition key, The pelletis a resistor, and when the key is inserted into the
m| | ignition lock cylinder it's value is 'read." if the value is recognized as being correct by the VATS module the vehicle will
|< Special start up. There are 15 different resistor pellets keys. There are two very thin and fragile wires connected to the VATS
on Note: ss | ignition lock cylinder that connects to the VATS module under the dash,
| When you disassemble the steering column, it is very important to observe how the VATS wires are attached to the
FE) | | inside of the column. When checking for the code on the ignition cylinder, it is also important to remember that the
im. wires connected to the cylinder are very delicate and great care should be taken as there isnot much room to slide the
Z| cylinder out, |

|= _ The panels have plastic hooks in them that lock into the door's sheet metal to hold the panel in place. The panel must be lifted up
| G2) to remove, and do not lift outward, like standard panels. There is usually a hidden ten-millimeterbolt behind the top portion ofthe | a
~| = _armrest/door pull. The bolt can be seen after gently snapping out the seat control face plate and then getting behind the inside
|| door release handle face plate. Unplug the panel light, to avoid breaking the wires attachedtoit, = ==3 = :

_ | Phillips head. Remove these screws. Remove the retaining screw on the edge of the door that holds the key cylinder in place,
___|__itmay be behind a black plastic plug. Remove the larger black plastic plug that contains the alarm connector and unsnap the =)
= | _ connector. The long tailpiece on the end of the key cylinder will have.a "wing tip' to it. The wing tip operates inside the latch
|_| assembly, and can only be removed by rotating the key in the lock cylinder.and gently pulling outward. Since you do not have a ==
|_| _ keyit will be necessary to gently pull out the key cylinder as far as it will go and then gently_rotate the latch assembly. It may work | Nn
|_| _ better to have the latch in the locked position or in the unlocked position. : 22>) JS see
